answer:
Sketch:
- Avg lifetime = 1/3% ≈ 33 months
- LTV = $120 × 80% × 33 ≈ $3,200
- LTV/CAC = 4x (good; 3x is the bar)
- CAC payback = $800 / $96 ≈ 8.3 months (strong; <12 is good)
- Concern: 3% monthly logo churn ≈ 31% annual — high for B2B

Answer: Yes, fundable on unit economics — 4x LTV/CAC and ~8-month payback clear standard VC benchmarks comfortably. The one red flag is churn: 3% monthly (~31% annually) signals weak retention or SMB-heavy mix, and investors will probe it hard. If you can show net revenue retention near or above 100% (expansion offsetting logo churn) and a credible path to cutting churn toward 1.5–2%, this is a solid Series A story. Growth rate and market size will matter as much as these ratios, though.
